item.title: 【Solving the Mystery: Why Java RSS Grows in Docker on M1 Macs】解开谜团：为什么 Java RSS 在 M1 Mac 上的 Docker 中增长
item.description:
【

The Problem

You're running a Java application in a Docker container on your M1 Mac. Everything works fine, but you notice something strange: The resident set size (RSS) keeps growing, even though your heap usage is stable. After hours of investigation, you find mysterious rwxp memory regions, each exactly 128 MB, accumulating in your process memory map.

What's causing this? Is it a memory leak? A JVM bug? Something else entirely?

】

问题

您正在 M1 Mac 上的 Docker 容器中运行 Java 应用程序。一切正常，但您注意到一些奇怪的事情：即使您的堆使用情况稳定，驻留集大小 (RSS) 仍在不断增长。经过几个小时的调查后，您发现神秘的 rwxp 内存区域（每个区域正好 128 MB）在进程内存映射中累积。

这是什么原因造成的？是内存泄漏吗？ JVM 错误？完全是别的东西吗？

item.title: 【The Art of Token Frugality in Generative AI Applications】生成式人工智能应用中代币节俭的艺术
item.description:
【

There was a time when token costs felt like rounding errors. A prototype making a few hundred calls a day, with a few cents here and there. That changes fast. When a generative AI (GenAI) application scales to thousands of users making multiple requests daily, token costs stop being a footnote and start being a line item that competes with infrastructure. The question is not whether to manage token consumption. It is whether you do so deliberately or by accident. 

This article organizes some of the methods for reducing token consumption in production GenAI and agentic AI applications. Though not an exhaustive list, it is an actionable set of principles to apply directly and generative enough to spark further ideas. After all, frugality is the mother of invention, and in the age of AI transformation, thinking carefully about where tokens go is not an optimization. It is a discipline.

】

曾经有一段时间，代币成本感觉像是舍入误差。一个原型每天要打几百个电话，到处都是几美分。这变化很快。当生成式人工智能 (GenAI) 应用程序扩展到每天发出多个请求的数千名用户时，代币成本不再是脚注，而是开始成为与基础设施竞争的项目。问题不在于是否管理代币消耗。这取决于你是故意还是无意。 

本文整理了一些在生产 GenAI 和代理 AI 应用程序中减少令牌消耗的方法。尽管不是详尽的清单，但它是一组可直接应用的可操作原则，并且足以激发更多想法。毕竟，节俭是发明之母，在人工智能变革的时代，仔细思考代币去向并不是一种优化。这是一门学科。

item.title: 【Has AI-Generated SQL Impacted Data Quality? We Reviewed 1,000 Incidents】AI 生成的 SQL 是否影响数据质量？我们审查了 1,000 起事件
item.description:
【

Code breaks data. At least it used to.

Data teams write SQL transformations to shape raw data for downstream use cases. When those queries change, they can rupture dependencies or alter metrics in unintended ways.

】

代码破坏数据。至少以前是这样。

数据团队编写 SQL 转换来为下游用例塑造原始数据。当这些查询发生变化时，它们可能会破坏依赖关系或以意想不到的方式改变指标。

item.title: 【Code Quality Had 5 Pillars. AI Broke 3 and Created 2 We Can’t Measure】代码质量有 5 个支柱。 AI 打破了 3 并创造了我们无法衡量的 2
item.description:
【

If you've been writing production software for more than a few years, you grew up with a gut sense of what "good code" meant beyond "it works." You could look at a pull request and feel whether the code was clean, or if the logic was going to be a nightmare to debug in six months. 

We formalized that gut sense into five things: readability, maintainability, security hygiene, documentation, and structural simplicity. We built tools to measure them, argued about them in code reviews, and underneath all of it sat an assumption so obvious that nobody bothered to say it out loud — a human wrote this, and that human can explain why every line is there.

】

如果您已经编写生产软件多年，您在成长过程中就会凭直觉了解“好代码”的含义，而不仅仅是“它有效”。您可以查看拉取请求，感受代码是否干净，或者逻辑是否会在六个月内成为调试的噩梦。 

我们将这种直觉形式化为五件事：可读性、可维护性、安全卫生、文档和结构简单性。我们构建了工具来衡量它们，在代码审查中对它们进行了争论，而在这一切的背后，隐藏着一个如此明显的假设，以至于没有人愿意大声说出来——这是一个人写的，并且人类可以解释为什么每一行都在那里。

item.title: 【You Secured the Code. Did You Secure the Model?】您保护了代码。你保护模型了吗？
item.description:
【

Your team just shipped an AI-powered feature. You scanned the code. Passed SAST. Reviewed the PR. Green across the board. 

But here’s what you probably didn't scan: the model weights. The agent framework. The dataset lineage. The MCP server that your agent calls at runtime. 

】

您的团队刚刚发布了一项人工智能支持的功能。您扫描了代码。通过SAST。审查了公关。全面绿色。 

但是您可能没有扫描到以下内容：模型权重。代理框架。数据集沿袭。您的代理在运行时调用的 MCP 服务器。

item.title: 【The 7 Pillars of Meeting Design: Transforming Expensive Conversations into Decision Assets】会议设计的 7 个支柱：将昂贵的对话转化为决策资产
item.description:
【

Software engineering prioritizes optimization, focusing on distributed systems, caching, cloud elasticity, observability, and AI-assisted development to boost productivity and speed. However, one of the most costly and overlooked inefficiencies is meeting culture. 

Research from Harvard Business Review, Atlassian, and Microsoft Work Trend Index consistently shows that professionals spend much of their week in meetings, many of which fail to produce decisions, clarity, or measurable outcomes. In software development, this issue is amplified, as meetings disrupt deep focus, a critical asset for engineers. A poorly structured one-hour meeting with ten engineers not only wastes an hour but also disrupts concentrated work, delays delivery, and increases organizational latency.

】

软件工程优先考虑优化，重点关注分布式系统、缓存、云弹性、可观察性和人工智能辅助开发，以提高生产力和速度。然而，成本最高且被忽视的效率低下之一是满足文化。 

来自哈佛商业评论、Atlassian和Microsoft 工作趋势指数始终显示，专业人员每周的大部分时间都花在会议上，其中许多会议未能做出决策、清晰度或可衡量的结果。在软件开发中，这个问题会被放大，因为会议会扰乱深度专注，而深度专注是工程师的一项重要资产。与 10 名工程师进行的一小时会议结构不良，不仅浪费一个小时，而且还会扰乱集中工作、延迟交付并增加组织延迟。

item.title: 【When Search Started Breaking at Scale: How We Chose the Right Search Engine】当搜索开始大规模突破时：我们如何选择正确的搜索引擎
item.description:
【

When we first built our search system, everything worked fine.

The data size was manageable, search responses were fast, and updates were happening as expected. Like many teams, we assumed that once a search engine is set up, it will continue to work as the system grows.

】

当我们第一次构建搜索系统时，一切都运行良好。

数据大小可控，搜索响应快速，并且更新按预期进行。与许多团队一样，我们假设搜索引擎一旦建立，它将随着系统的发展而继续工作。

item.title: 【Scalable Support Request Analysis Using Embeddings, HDBSCAN, and Tiny LLMs】使用嵌入、HDBSCAN 和 Tiny LLM 进行可扩展支持请求分析
item.description:
【

Data Exploration

Analyze the historical data to understand data quality, recurring key phrases, noise, and other patterns. Also, examine meta-attributes such as manual tagging, assigned department, assigned personnel, etc. Use spaCy or any other library to identify the most common words. This will indicate which words need to be masked, replaced, or normalized.

Data Cleansing and Enrichment

Identify domain-specific noise and aliases, and define regex rules to remove or standardize them.

】

数据探索

分析历史数据以了解数据质量、重复出现的关键短语、噪音和其他模式。另外，检查元属性，例如手动标记、分配的部门、分配的人员等。使用 spaCy 或任何其他库来识别最常见的单词。这将指示哪些单词需要屏蔽、替换或规范化。

数据清理和充实

识别特定于域的噪声和别名，并定义正则表达式规则以删除或标准化它们。

item.title: 【DuckDB for Python Developers】面向 Python 开发人员的 DuckDB
item.description:
【

If you have ever tried to run a quick aggregation on a 3GB CSV file in pandas, you know the ritual: wait for it to load into the memory, watch your RAM climb, maybe get a Memory Error, then reach for something heavier — a Postgres instance, a Spark cluster, a cloud warehouse. It's a lot of infrastructure for what should be a five-minute analysis. 

DuckDB exists to break that cycle. It's an analytical database that runs entirely in process, requires zero setup, and can query CSV files, Parquet, and pandas DataFrames directly — often faster than tools that cost thousands of dollars a month to run. This post is for Python developers who work with data and want a sharper tool in their kit.

】

如果您曾经尝试过在 pandas 中对 3GB CSV 文件运行快速聚合，您就会知道这个仪式：等待它加载到内存中，观察 RAM 爬升，也许会出现内存错误，然后找到更重的东西 - Postgres 实例、Spark 集群、云仓库。对于五分钟的分析来说，需要大量的基础设施。 

DuckDB 的存在就是为了打破这个循环。它是一个完全在进程中运行的分析数据库，需要零设置，并且可以直接查询 CSV 文件、Parquet 和 pandas DataFrame，通常比每月花费数千美元运行的工具更快。这篇文章适用于处理数据并希望在他们的工具包中使用更锋利的工具的 Python 开发人员。

item.title: 【AI in Software Development: A Mirror, Not a Magic Wand】软件开发中的人工智能：一面镜子，而不是一根魔杖
item.description:
【

“AI’s inflection point has arrived.” This statement reflects how deeply artificial intelligence is embedding itself into our daily lives. For software developers, that reality is unavoidable. Whether you’re using GitHub Copilot for small productivity gains or experimenting with more advanced agentic workflows, AI-assisted development is here to stay — and learning to use it well is quickly becoming essential.

“AI’s inflection point has arrived.”

】

“人工智能的拐点已经到来。”这句话反映了人工智能正在多么深入地融入我们的日常生活。对于软件开发人员来说，这个现实是不可避免的。无论您是使用 GitHub Copilot 来小幅提高生产力，还是尝试更先进的代理工作流程，人工智能辅助开发都将继续存在，并且学习如何良好地使用它很快就变得至关重要。

<块引用>

“人工智能的拐点已经到来。”
